message:
fix(determinism): canonicalize interference metadata entries for order-invariant hashing

InterferenceMetadata::build() now canonicalizes entries before computing
counts and content hashes:

- canonicalize_entry: normalizes rule pair orientation so (rule_a, rule_b)
  always has rule_a <= rule_b lexicographically (swaps if needed)
- canonicalize_entries: maps each entry through canonicalize_entry, then
  sorts and deduplicates the list

This ensures (r2, r1, PatternConflict) and (r1, r2, PatternConflict) are
treated as the same interference, and duplicate entries after normalization
are removed. Without this, different discovery orders would produce
different metadata hashes, causing spurious mismatches across runs.

New tests:
- interference_metadata_canonicalizes_rule_orientation_and_duplicates:
  verifies swapped rule names collapse to one entry
- interference_metadata_hash_is_stable_across_entry_order: verifies
  different orderings produce identical metadata and content hashes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
